summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday): **THE BEIRUT RED-LINE THE JUNE 7 BRIEF CALLED 'HELD' REVERSED WITHIN HOURS — ISRAEL STRUCK A 'HEZBOLLAH COMMAND CENTRE' IN BEIRUT'S DAHIYEH SUBURB (≥2 KILLED, 11-20 WOUNDED, FIRST CAPITAL STRIKE SINCE THE RENEWED TRUCE), IRAN ANSWERED WITH ITS FIRST BALLISTIC SALVO SINCE APRIL 8, ISRAEL STRUCK DEEP INTO IRAN JUNE 8 — THEN BOTH SIDES STOOD DOWN WITHIN 24H UNDER TRUMP PRESSURE. ISRAEL'S FREEDOM OF ACTION NARROWED TO LEBANON, WHERE THE ESCALATION PROMPTLY WENT: FIRST-EVER WHOLE-CITY EVACUATION ORDER FOR TYRE (JUNE 8), THEN THE DEADLIEST TYRE RAID OF THE WAR (JUNE 9, ≥8 KILLED) — CONTINUING AFTER THE ISRAEL-IRAN SUSPENSION.** (CONFIRMED ACTION — ISW, Al Jazeera, NPR, Axios) Sequence: Hezbollah fired rockets near Yiftach -> Israel struck what Netanyahu/Katz called a 'Hezbollah command centre' in Beirut's southern Dahiyeh suburb (≥2 killed, 11-20 wounded; first attack on the capital since the truce was renewed) — crossing the EXACT geographic red-line the June 7 brief flagged as holding. Iran responded ~22:00 June 7 with its first ballistic-missile salvo on Israel since the April 8 ceasefire. Israel then (June 8) struck deep into Iran (Tehran/Isfahan/Tabriz/Kermanshah/Mahshahr); BOTH sides stood down within 24h after Trump forced Netanyahu to call off a second wave ('Israel would be on its own,' REPORTED); the IRGC announced a suspension; Netanyahu vowed to respond 'with force' if attacked. The Israeli press is openly humiliating: Haaretz 'Netanyahu's weakness on full display,' Israel reduced to 'utter dependence on the United States' accepting 'Trump's dictates' on both Iran and Lebanon; ToI 'PM called off major Iran strike after Trump warned Israel would be on its own.' CRUCIALLY Israel's residual freedom of action narrowed to Lebanon and the escalation went there: a first-ever evacuation order for the ENTIRE city of Tyre (June 8), then the deadliest Tyre raid of the war June 9 (≥8 killed) — continuing AFTER the Israel-Iran mutual suspension (the two axes are decoupled in Israeli targeting, exactly as Katz signalled). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: the 'expanded operation against Hezbollah' trigger (ACTIVE) STAYS ACTIVE and INTENSIFIED — the whole-city Tyre evacuation + deadliest Tyre raid are intensification-within-active. The Beirut red-line (a watched escalation threshold flagged 'held' on June 7) was CROSSED June 7 by a CONFIRMED Israeli strike on Dahiyeh — documented as a confirmed action; it produced the first Iranian salvo since April but BOTH sides stood down within 24h, so it did NOT become full-scale war resumption. The 'Israeli unilateral kinetic action on Iran' trigger (PAST its 2026-06-05 deadline, left at watching): Israel DID strike Iran June 8 (CONFIRMED) — but with US sign-off initially then a Trump-forced halt; it fired in spirit (Israel struck Iran) yet was immediately capped by Washington — left at watching as a residual marker, the kinetic action was real but US-capped, not an autonomous unilateral campaign. Day 100 / 2026-06-07 (Sunday): **ISRAEL KEPT STRIKING SOUTHERN LEBANON UNDER THE FRAYING TRUCE — SATURDAY STRIKES KILLED NINE INCLUDING THREE LEBANESE *ARMY* PERSONNEL — WHILE ABSORBING ITS OWN CASUALTIES: THREE SOLDIERS KILLED IN FOUR DAYS. BEIRUT WAS NOT STRUCK.** (intel A2, CONFIRMED ACTION) Saturday 6 June IDF airstrikes killed nine (a strike on Saksakiyah killed six civilians), including three Lebanese ARMY members — undercutting the Beirut government Israel signed the truce with. Defence Minister Katz reiterated operations 'will continue,' demanded a demilitarised zone, and said Israel 'would not be withdrawing.' Against that, Hezbollah killed three Israeli soldiers since the truce (Capt. Lemberg Thu, Sgt. Yaari Fri, Capt. Galma Sat) — the Israeli press is now covering a war that is COSTING the army, historically a tightener on an open-ended Lebanon operation even as the government stays publicly defiant. The strikes stayed in the SOUTH (Tyre/Sidon/Saksakiyah/Nabatieh); BEIRUT WAS NOT STRUCK. **TRIGGER (June 7): the 'Second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during the 45-day ceasefire window' trigger (active_fired) now stands at THREE IDF KIA in four days; the 'expanded operation against Hezbollah' trigger (active) is intensifying-within-active — both SUB-BEIRUT, no new activation.** Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5-6 June). **ISRAEL KEPT STRIKING SOUTHERN LEBANON UNDER THE RENEWED CEASEFIRE — ≥21 KILLED FRIDAY ACROSS TYRE/SIDON/NABATIEH — AND ORDERED EVACUATIONS NORTH OF THE LITANI, BUT DELIBERATELY STAYED OUT OF BEIRUT.** Despite the 3-June agreement to renew the ceasefire with 'pilot/safe zones,' the IDF kept operating: Israeli strikes killed AT LEAST 21 in southern Lebanon Friday 5 June (CONFIRMED — Al Jazeera, CBS) — seven in Tyre overnight, plus a Sidon municipal council member, two Syrian children and a paramedic in Zebdine — and Israel ordered residents of three villages north of the Litani to EVACUATE ahead of expected operations. Operationally defiant, consistent with Katz's prior 'there is no ceasefire in Lebanon.' CRUCIALLY the strikes stayed OUT of Beirut (Tyre/Sidon/Nabatieh, not the capital) — the red-line Iran (Araghchi) tied to a 'full-scale resumption' was NOT crossed. On the IRAN front Israel was NOT the actor in any new kinetic event this window; its energy stayed in Lebanon. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 6)**: the 'Second IDF KIA in the 45-day window' trigger stays active_fired (no new threshold). The expanded-Lebanon-operation trigger (shared) STAYS ACTIVE — a fraying, Hezbollah-rejected ceasefire is not a resolution. The Israeli-unilateral-kinetic-action-on-Iran trigger (by 2026-06-05) is now PAST its deadline UNTRIPPED — no Israeli strike on Iran across the window. NO trigger moves to active — Beirut was NOT struck, the dead are in Lebanon not Israeli civilians. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **ISRAEL KEPT FIGHTING UNDER A 'CEASEFIRE' HEZBOLLAH REJECTED — STRIKES KILLED >=4 IN LEBANON AND A UNIFIL PEACEKEEPER, AND IT LOST CAPT. LEMBERG, THE FIRST SOLDIER KILLED SINCE THE ANNOUNCEMENT.** A ceasefire was announced out of the 4th round (to take effect early Thursday 4 June), but Hezbollah publicly REJECTED it and the IDF kept operating: Israeli strikes killed at least 4 in Lebanon (Boston Globe), a Serbian UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed (Salvadoran + Spanish wounded) by mortars near Marjayoun (UNIFIL via NPR), and **Capt. Eitan Shmuel Lemberg (21) was killed by a Hezbollah anti-tank missile** — the first IDF soldier killed since the announced ceasefire (CONFIRMED — IDF via ToI). Israeli media centred on the Lemberg KIA and continued strikes 'despite the Washington framing,' consistent with Katz's flat 'there is no ceasefire in Lebanon' — an army still fighting under a truce Hezbollah rejects. The Trump-Netanyahu profane rift (the 3-June 'leash') is now rear-view but unresolved; Trump's 'Hezbollah did not reject / progress is being made' gloss is contradicted by Hezbollah and by the body count. On the IRAN front Israel was NOT the actor in any new kinetic event — the Gulf de-escalated (no new Iran barrage 4 June), so Israeli energy stayed in Lebanon. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 4)**: the 'Second IDF KIA in the 45-day window' trigger stays active_fired (the Lemberg KIA is a further in-window combat death, not a new threshold). The expanded-Lebanon-operation trigger (shared) STAYS ACTIVE — an announced-but-rejected-and-violated ceasefire is not a resolution. The Israeli-unilateral-kinetic-action-on-Iran trigger (by 2026-06-05) STAYS WATCHING and is now at/near expiry — no Israeli strike on Iran across the window; the Gulf de-escalation makes an imminent unilateral strike less likely, not more. No trigger moves to active (the casualties are a soldier + a peacekeeper, not US/allied-military, and not Israeli civilians). PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **THE TRUMP-NETANYAHU RIFT WENT FULLY PUBLIC AND PROFANE; ISRAEL KEPT BOMBING SOUTH LEBANON (≥8 KILLED) BUT HELD THE BEIRUT CAP.** Trump confirmed (via Axios) he told Netanyahu in their 1 June call 'You're f---ing crazy. You'd be in prison if not for me,' driven by anger that Netanyahu's Lebanon escalation was imperilling the Iran talks — the clearest on-record US-Israel daylight of the cycle. Netanyahu's counter-framing (3 June, CNBC): he and Trump have 'tactical disagreements' but a 'great relationship' and agree Hezbollah 'must be disarmed.' Israeli media (Haaretz, strong military sourcing) carried Netanyahu's 'operations in Lebanon continue as planned' and Katz's flat 'there is no ceasefire in Lebanon' — the security cabinet publicly resisting the US-imposed Beirut cap even as it complies with it; Israel held off Beirut's southern suburbs but kept bombing south Lebanon (≥8 killed) and eased civil-defence restrictions in northern Israel (a de-escalation marker). The 4th round of Israel-Lebanon talks convened in DC (2-3 June). Tone: defiant on substance, visibly boxed in by Trump on Beirut and on not blowing up the Iran track. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 3)**: the 'Second IDF KIA in the 45-day window' trigger stays active_fired. The expanded-Lebanon-operation trigger (shared) STAYS ACTIVE. The Israeli-unilateral-kinetic-action-on-Iran trigger (by 2026-06-05) STAYS WATCHING — no Israeli strike on Iran (the 3 June kinetic action was US-Iran). PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **ISRAEL HELD OFF THE BEIRUT-SUBURBS STRIKE UNDER TRUMP PRESSURE BUT KEPT WARRING IN THE SOUTH — A NEW GOLANI KIA, AND AN OPEN TRUMP-NETANYAHU RIFT. After Trump asked Netanyahu not to strike Beirut's southern suburbs (1 June) and announced an Israel-Hezbollah 'halt,' Israel held off the Beirut attack but did NOT independently confirm a ceasefire — Netanyahu's office said south-Lebanon operations 'would continue' if Hezbollah keeps targeting Israeli civilians, and Axios reported a 'tense phone conversation' between Trump and Netanyahu over the continued strikes. On 2 June Israel kept striking south Lebanon while holding off Beirut (CONFIRMED — US News/Reuters, Al-Monitor, UN News). A new IDF Golani Brigade soldier was killed and five wounded in a clash with Hezbollah (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel) — a fresh in-window combat death. Cross-border de-escalation signals: Israel intercepted two projectiles overnight, Hezbollah ran no new cross-border rocket attacks (vs 50+ on 31 May), and schools reopened in northern Israel. The Israeli press is reporting a leader publicly accepting a US-imposed limit on ONE target (Beirut) while refusing a comprehensive halt — watch for the Haaretz-style elite-dissent track to resurface now that the campaign produced a fresh combat death AND an open Trump-Netanyahu rift. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 2)**: the 'Second IDF KIA in the 45-day window' trigger MOVES to active_fired — the literal threshold is crossed by the confirmed Golani KIA (plus prior in-window deaths), though its PREDICTED consequence (Pentagon-track collapse) has NOT materialized (4th round DC talks convened 2-3 June; rocket intensity dropped). The expanded-Lebanon-operation trigger (shared) STAYS ACTIVE. The Israeli-unilateral-kinetic-action-on-Iran trigger (by 2026-06-05) STAYS WATCHING — no Israeli strike on Iran this window (the kinetic action was US-Iran). PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **ISRAEL CROSSED THE BEIRUT RED LINE — NETANYAHU CONFIRMED IDF 'OPERATING IN BEIRUT AND THE BEKAA' AND ACROSS THE LITANI ON 29 MAY ('tactical victory') — THE FIRST BEIRUT ACTION IN ~3 WEEKS AND A NEW CROSSING OF WASHINGTON'S EXPLICIT WARNING (issued to protect the Iran talks). Israel issued sweeping evacuation orders for ~7 southern towns south of the Zahrani; the Lebanese MoPH reported 142 killed/72h (cumulative ~3,355). IDF combat deaths reached 24 (RECONCILED UPWARD from the 28 May '22' baseline; a Givati Brigade soldier killed + 2 reservists wounded by a Hezbollah drone on 27 May). The Beirut operations ran SIMULTANEOUSLY with the first-ever direct Israel-Lebanon military talks at the Pentagon on 29 May — Israel's military and political tracks are one coercive strategy. Israeli elite dissent is now public: Haaretz ran 'Israel's Wars of Deception: Escalation Masquerading as Strategy' (treat as opinion, not intelligence — but a marker of center-left dissent over the Lebanon expansion as casualties rise). On the IRAN front Israel was NOT the actor in any new kinetic event this window; its energy stayed in Lebanon, so the Israeli-unilateral-Iran-strike trigger STAYS watching. The expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (fired May 27).** Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **THE LEBANON GROUND WAR GRINDS ON — ISRAEL TOOK ITS FIRST GROUND-ENGAGEMENT IDF KIA (22 TOTAL); ISRAEL'S IRAN-FRONT ENERGY IS LARGELY RHETORICAL THIS WINDOW. Israeli forces continued expanding ground operations beyond the Yellow Line and north of the Litani on May 27, fighting Hezbollah around Zawtar Sharqieh and Yohmor al-Shaqif (the IDF had not established control of Zawtar Sharqieh by afternoon); Hebrew media frame it as pushing Hezbollah north to reduce explosive-drone threats to northern Israel. The IDF struck near the Qaraoun Dam (Litani River Authority: no direct damage). One IDF soldier was killed and another seriously wounded by a Hezbollah drone on May 27 — the first ground-engagement IDF KIA, raising the IDF military-death baseline to 22 since March 2; it is being absorbed as a cost of the operation, not a constraint on it. NO Beirut strike was reported (Washington warned Israel against striking in Beirut to protect the Iran talks; targeted assassinations there remain notionally permitted). NEAR-TERM: a Lebanese-Israeli-American military coordination meeting at the Pentagon on May 29 (tomorrow), ahead of the fourth round of Israel-Lebanon peace talks June 2-3 in Washington.** On the Iran front, Israel's role this window is rhetorical (it was NOT the actor in the May 28 US-Iran exchange); the Israeli-unilateral-Iran-strike trigger STAYS watching. The expanded-operation trigger STAYS ACTIVE (fired May 27). PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 of Hormuz blockade (Wednesday May 27). **ISRAEL LAUNCHED THE EXPANDED GROUND OPERATION ACROSS THE LITANI — THE LEBANON TRIGGER FIRES ON BOTH LIMBS (CONFIRMED).** Israel expanded its ground operation beyond the security zone and ACROSS THE LITANI RIVER in southern Lebanon; Netanyahu: 'The IDF are operating with large forces on the ground and seizing strategic areas' (CONFIRMED — Euronews May 27, Algemeiner May 26, Times of Israel liveblogs). Overnight Israel struck 100+ Hezbollah sites (storage, command centres, observation posts); Lebanon's health ministry reported at least 31 killed including children (Mashghara ~12; a village north of the Litani ~12), with evacuation warnings for Nabatiyeh province (CONFIRMED — Lebanon Health Ministry via Euronews). Washington APPROVED the expansion but WARNED Israel against striking in Beirut to avoid damaging the Iran talks; a senior Israeli official said targeted assassinations in Beirut are permitted 'if an operational opportunity presents itself' (CONFIRMED). The May-25 trigger (US greenlights OR Israel launches an expanded operation against Hezbollah within 14 days, by 2026-06-08) is now satisfied on BOTH limbs by confirmed action → moved to active. Israeli source-tone hardened to an open PREFERENCE for a return to war in Iran (NPR/Jerusalem Post) — yet Israel's kinetic energy has fully rotated to the LEBANON front, so the Israeli-unilateral-Iran-strike trigger stays watching. This escalation jeopardises the separate US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire track (next talks June 2-3) and directly collides with Iran's deal precondition that any agreement 'end the war on all fronts including Lebanon.' Context: Hezbollah fired 1,000+ drones and 700+ rockets at Israel over the preceding eight days (REPORTED tally). PRIOR (May 25): Day 87 of Hormuz blockade (Monday May 25). **ISRAEL HARDENED + LEBANON FRONT HOT — THE 'ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE HOLDS' FRAMING IS RETIRED.** In a one-day hardening from Sunday's Gamliel 'wait-and-see', Netanyahu convened an EMERGENCY SECURITY CABINET on the emerging US-Iran framework; official sources called it 'a bad deal' handing Tehran 'strategic leverage through the Strait of Hormuz,' and far-right ministers 'demanded he renew military operations in Lebanon' (cabinet convened CONFIRMED — multiple; 'bad deal' characterisation + Lebanon-operation demands REPORTED — Voice of Emirates citing Israeli sources). Opposition leader Lapid called the deal 'a disaster' (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel, WaPo). Trump reportedly backed Israel's 'freedom of action against threats on all fronts, including Lebanon' (REPORTED). **The Lebanon front is now HOT and the day's most acute deal-breaker**: a senior US official (Channel 12 + Axios via TOI) signalled Washington could GREENLIGHT A LARGER ISRAELI OPERATION against Hezbollah, which has reportedly fired OVER 1,000 DRONES AND OVER 700 ROCKETS at Israel over EIGHT DAYS; IDF Northern Command chief Maj. Gen. Rafi Milo said the command is 'at war' (campaign CONFIRMED; exact tally REPORTED) and the IDF has drawn up expanded Lebanon plans. The April-16 (extended) Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is FRAGILE AND ACTIVELY VIOLATED — not 'holding.' New trigger added at `watching`: US greenlights OR Israel launches an expanded operation against Hezbollah within 14 days (by 2026-06-08). Iran claimed it downed an Israeli 'Orbiter' surveillance drone over Hormozgan May 24; IDF 'not familiar with the incident' (CLAIMED — low significance, no weight change). No expanded ground operation launched yet (REPORTED/PREPARATION); the Israeli unilateral-Iran-strike trigger remains `watching` with operational floor intact. PRIOR (May 24): **GAMLIEL (LIKUD, NATIONAL SECURITY CABINET) PUBLICLY CONFIRMED ISRAEL 'WAIT-AND-SEE' POSTURE ON ARMY RADIO — CABINET TONE IS WATCH AND VERIFY, NOT OPERATIONAL PREP. NETANYAHU TOLD TRUMP IN SATURDAY-NIGHT CALL ISRAEL 'WILL CONTINUE TO TAKE MILITARY ACTION AS IT SEES FIT, IRRESPECTIVE OF ANY US-IRAN PEACE DEAL' (TOI SOURCES) — DOMESTIC HARDLINE POSITIONING WITHOUT OPERATIONAL PREPARATION. TRUMP'S PRIVATE ASSURANCE TO NETANYAHU: 'WILL NOT SIGN ANY FINAL AGREEMENT WITHOUT IRAN DISMANTLING ENTIRE NUCLEAR PROGRAM AND REMOVING ALL ENRICHED URANIUM' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS US ADMIN PUBLIC 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED TO DISPOSAL.' NO HAARETZ/CHANNEL 12/13 IMMINENT-STRIKE REPORTING REPRODUCED SUNDAY. ISRAELI UNILATERAL-RISK TRIGGER DAY 3 OF 14 (BY 2026-06-05) — OPERATIONAL FLOOR HOLDS.** (1) **Gamliel (Likud, Netanyahu's national security cabinet) on Army Radio Sunday** (CONFIRMED — Yahoo/AP, Inquirer, Times of Israel): Israel taking a 'wait-and-see' approach. First on-record cabinet-level public articulation of Israeli posture since the leaked deal text. (2) **Netanyahu Saturday-night call with Trump (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: he told Trump Israel 'will continue to take military action as it sees fit, irrespective of any US-Iran peace deal' and explicitly emphasised 'freedom of action against threats in all arenas, including Lebanon.' Netanyahu's public position: 'remained unchanged: Iran will not have nuclear weapons' with explicit demand for dismantlement of enrichment sites. (3) **Trump-Netanyahu private call assurance (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: Trump told Netanyahu he 'will not sign any final agreement without the conditions that Iran dismantle its entire nuclear program and remove all enriched uranium from the country.' **This private assurance directly contradicts the US admin's public framing that Iran has 'in principle agreed' to disposal — either Trump is double-tracking with Netanyahu vs the press, or the admin source over-stated Iranian acceptance.** (4) **No operational preparation by IDF, no Haaretz/Channel 12/13 imminent-strike reporting reproduced May 24, no movement on Riklin-claimed 'revealed plans'** — the Israeli unilateral kinetic trigger (Day 3 of 14, by June 5) remains `watching` with operational floor intact. (5) **Israeli media is moving from 'Bibi's hair on fire' framing (May 21-22) toward 'watch and verify' posture** — neither escalating to operational preparation nor accommodating the US deal narrative. This is more dangerous than the prior framing because it preserves Israel's optionality without forcing a public commitment. (6) **Issacharoff thesis (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has NOT publicly pivoted** from 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains largely intact underground' — but no escalation toward unilateral-action prep argument today. (7) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 10 of 45 extension** intact; Pentagon-led security track May 29 launch on calendar; no second IDF KIA inside Litani-area scope (May 14 Negev Dagan remains the only ceasefire-window casualty). PRIOR (May 23): NETANYAHU CHAIRED SECOND SECURITY CABINET MEETING ON IRAN ACROSS THE WEEK BUT ISRAELI OFFICIALS PUBLICLY SAY 'NO DECISION ON IRAN ATTACK; ISRAEL, US IN TUNE ON STRATEGIC CHALLENGES' — TIMES OF ISRAEL EXPLICITLY CONTRADICTS OPERATIONAL-READINESS FRAMING. SINGLE-SOURCE-ANCHOR RIKLIN TV CLAIM OF 'REVEALED CONFIDENTIAL PLANS' UNCORROBORATED BY MAINSTREAM ISRAELI PRINT OUTLETS. ANHA/HAWAR NEWS 'NETANYAHU APPROVES ATTACK PLAN' = SINGLE-SOURCE DIASPORA/KURDISH, CLAIMED ONLY.** Across May 21-23: (1) Netanyahu chaired SECOND security cabinet meeting on Iran across the week (per Times of Israel + Al Jazeera May 21-22 + Haaretz May 23). (2) Israeli TV anchor Shimon Riklin appeared on Hebrew-language broadcast to claim he could 'reveal confidential plans' about renewed attack on Tehran including location of 'a uranium storage facility that could be targeted' (REPORTED — single-source via Al Jazeera May 21 referring to Israeli media; uncorroborated by other Israeli outlets). (3) **A separate Times of Israel report ('Israeli official: No decision on Iran attack; Israel, US in tune on strategic challenges') explicitly contradicts the operational-readiness framing** — official Israeli government line is no decision. (4) **The Kurdish-language ANHA/Hawar News headline 'Netanyahu approves Israeli attack plan on Iran' is single-source diaspora/Kurdish-aligned outlet that has not been independently corroborated by mainstream Israeli media (Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post) — treated as CLAIMED only, NOT REPORTED.** (5) **Issacharoff thesis (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has not pivoted from his 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains intact underground' framing** — strengthens with Al Arabiya text's omission of nuclear dismantlement but Issacharoff has NOT amplified unilateral-action prep argument today. (6) **Haaretz May 23 confirms US deal-track activity dominates the Israeli media agenda, not unilateral-action prep**. (7) **Israeli unilateral-risk trigger Day 2 of 14 (by 2026-06-05)**: posture hardening rhetorically but operational floor holds — same pattern as Houthi Haifa rhetoric (rhetoric-without-action). (8) **Al Jazeera continues 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' framing into May 23** — Qatari editorial alignment + Haaretz May 23 confirming US deal-track dominance = the dominant Israeli media frame today is that Israel is WATCHING the US deal track move, not preparing its own kinetic action. This is structurally a notable shift from the May 21-22 'Bibi's hair on fire' + Al Jazeera 'Israel pushes for war' framing — the operational evidence of Israeli unilateral preparation has not surfaced even as rhetorical frustration is on the record. (9) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 9 of 45**: extension architecture (Pentagon-led security track starting May 29) operationally intact. **The May 22 framing holds**: Israeli political establishment frustration is broad enough to leak (Axios 'hair on fire,' Jerusalem Post echo), but no formal cabinet approval of unilateral action confirmed, and Israeli official rebuttal exists. PRIOR (May 22): **TRUMP-NETANYAHU 'DIFFICULT' MAY 20 CALL — SOURCES DESCRIBED NETANYAHU'S 'HAIR WAS ON FIRE' AFTER (AXIOS SCOOP MAY 20). TRUMP PUBLICLY: NETANYAHU 'WILL DO WHATEVER I WANT HIM TO DO.' THE CLEANEST PUBLICLY STATED US-ISRAEL DIVERGENCE OF THE CYCLE. CNN POLITICS MAY 20: 'TRUMP AND NETANYAHU DIVERGE ON IRAN WAR'S FUTURE IN TENSE PHONE CALL.'** Across May 20-22: (1) **Axios scoop (May 20, Barak Ravid byline)**: Trump-Netanyahu held 'difficult' phone call about Iran ceasefire talks; sources described Netanyahu's 'hair was on fire' after — Netanyahu told Trump he believed delaying expected attacks was a mistake and that the president should continue as planned. (2) **Trump May 20 White House (Times of Israel May 20 liveblog primary, CBS, CNN)**: 'in no hurry' to reach a deal; Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do'; 'open to a limited Iran deal that would only open the Strait of Hormuz and extend the ceasefire.' **This is structurally larger than May 18 Haaretz 'As Trump Hesitates' framing because Trump explicitly subordinated Netanyahu ON THE RECORD.** (3) **CNN Politics May 20: 'Trump and Netanyahu diverge on Iran war's future in tense phone call'** — explicit divergence framing from Democrat-aligned outlet. (4) **Al Jazeera May 21 (Qatari editorial alignment): 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited'** — Gulf framing of Israel as deal-spoiler. **Netanyahu reportedly chaired a SECOND security cabinet meeting on Iran across the week** (per Iran International May 19, Al Jazeera May 21, Times of Israel May 20-22) with 'strong desire in the upper echelons of the Israeli government for renewed military action and mounting frustration that Trump is continuing to allow what they say is Iran's diplomatic foot-dragging.' (5) **Issacharoff thesis (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has NOT publicly pivoted from his 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains intact underground' framing** — that framing is strengthened by the leaked Al Arabiya deal text's omission of nuclear dismantlement (B1 intel), providing the publicly-stated Israeli intelligence case against accepting the Pakistan-mediated text. (6) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 8 of 45**: IDF airstrikes targeted Nabatieh (May 22) and al-Haniyya (May 21) per Haaretz wire imagery. Lebanese health ministry casualty tracking continues from prior week's 18-killed/124-wounded reading. NO second IDF KIA across May 19-22 — May 14 Negev Dagan KIA remains the only ceasefire-window casualty inside Litani-area scope. Hezbollah has NOT publicly claimed retaliatory mortar/rocket fire May 19-22. Ceasefire extension architecture (Pentagon-led security track starting May 29) operationally intact. **The most consequential signal**: Israeli media is no longer running unified anti-deal coverage — Jerusalem Post and Axios primary-source the 'Bibi's hair was on fire' framing (both right-of-center outlets willing to run unflattering Netanyahu framing reveals political establishment frustration is now broad enough to leak). **This means either Israeli unilateral kinetic action is being prepared as fact (Haaretz 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares' pattern at higher amplitude), OR the Israeli establishment is operationally accepting a deal it loses on.** Trigger added: 'Israel unilateral kinetic action within US-postponement window or in response to deal text leak (by 2026-06-05).' PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A3 TRUMP-NETANYAHU PHONE CALL SUNDAY + ISRAELI SECURITY CABINET MEETING + 'CLOCK IS TICKING' TRUMP POST + TUESDAY MAY 19 NSC MEETING SCHEDULED** — Day 1 of 6 remaining in NYT 'as soon as next week' window. Trump and Netanyahu spoke by phone Sunday May 17 ahead of limited Israeli security cabinet meeting; both sides framed call as discussion of 'renewing the war with Iran.' Netanyahu addressed Cabinet: **'our eyes are also open' regarding Iran, 'prepared for any scenario.'** Small security cabinet attendees: FM Sa'ar, Defense Min Katz, Finance Min Smotrich, NSC Min Ben Gvir, Shas chair Deri = war-decision quorum. Trump posted 'for Iran, the clock is ticking' on Truth Social later Sunday (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). Trump expected to meet top national security team Tuesday May 19 (REPORTED via Haaretz/Times of Israel May 18; no White House public announcement through Monday morning IST). **Haaretz May 18 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares for Renewed Iran Strikes' is the cleanest publicly stated US-Israel-divergence framing of the cycle** — Israeli defense establishment positioning for unilateral preparation while Trump described as hesitating despite NYT preparation reporting and his own escalatory rhetoric. Haaretz with strongest IDF military sourcing — framing at minimum the senior IDF reading; at maximum a deliberate Israeli leak intended to *pressure* Trump toward action. **A former senior Israeli intelligence officer in Haaretz piece publicly argues Iran's nuclear infrastructure and missile production remain 'largely intact underground'** — deliberate argument for renewed kinetic action with the strategic concession that Iran may now conclude weaponization is the only deterrent. **A8 ISRAEL-LEBANON DAY 4 OF 45-DAY CEASEFIRE EXTENSION**: Lebanese health ministry reports 18 killed + 124 wounded in Israeli strikes past day (Haaretz live blog May 17, ms.now); cumulative ~2,987 dead / ~9,236 wounded since March 2 (additive). **IDF reported NO new injuries through Monday morning IST; no second IDF KIA confirmed.** Litani-induced Israeli-soldier-KIA trigger remains `active_fired` from Day 2 of 14 (Dagan mortar killing); May 16 'second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during 45-day ceasefire extension window' trigger remains UNTRIPPED. Both sides continue to operate within unwritten kinetic floor (strikes) and ceiling (no escalation to mass-casualty IDF events). **Pentagon security track May 29 launch remains on calendar.** Al Jazeera continues scare-quoted 'ceasefire' framing on Israel-Lebanon. **The dominant Israeli media tone is operational urgency: this is the strike window, and US hesitation is the problem.** **PRIOR (May 17): KINETIC AXIS HARDENS PUBLICLY**: NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) REPORTED that **Israel and the US are carrying out 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran, possibly as soon as next week'** — options include (i) commando operation to extract nuclear material believed buried under rubble at struck nuclear sites; (ii) ground forces to seize Kharg Island.
